note: All values below were computed with a real YAML parser (PyYAML safe_load) and strategic-merge semantics. They are deterministic and offline; nothing depends on the network.

conflicts.detail: LOG_LEVEL is defined twice in the same mapping: first "info", then "debug". YAML keeps the LAST occurrence.
conflicts.why_it_matters: A reader skimming the top of the file sees LOG_LEVEL=info, but the object actually carries LOG_LEVEL=debug. Silent key shadowing.

conflicts.detail: container[0] (orders) defines resources with anchor &default-resources; container[1] (metrics-sidecar) reuses it via *default-resources. The two containers therefore share an identical resource block (requests cpu 250m / mem 256Mi, limits cpu 500m / mem 512Mi).
conflicts.why_it_matters: The alias is not a separate copy — editing the anchor changes both containers. A correct audit must note that the sidecar inherits the orders container's resources via the alias, not as an independent value.

conflicts.detail: TLS_ENABLED value is the unquoted token `no`. Under YAML 1.1 (and PyYAML) this parses as the boolean False, not the string "no". Kubernetes env var values must be strings, so this is also schema-invalid (a bool where a string is required).
conflicts.why_it_matters: The value silently becomes a boolean. It must be quoted ("no" or "false") to be a valid env value; the bug is invisible unless you actually parse the file.

conflicts.detail: base image is registry.example.com/orders:1.4.0. staging patch.yaml re-states image :1.4.0, but staging kustomization.yaml has an images transformer setting newTag 1.5.0-rc.1. The kustomize images transformer is applied after patches, so it WINS.
conflicts.why_it_matters: The image tag literally written in the patch (1.4.0) is NOT what gets deployed; the transformer overrides it. Reading only the patch misleads.

conflicts.detail: prod patch.yaml pins image :1.4.2, but prod kustomization.yaml images transformer sets newTag 1.6.0. Transformer wins.
conflicts.why_it_matters: Same trap as staging: the explicit image line in the prod patch (1.4.2) is overridden by the transformer (1.6.0).

conflicts.detail: prod patch.yaml pins Deployment spec.replicas: 5, while prod/hpa.yaml defines an HPA targeting the same Deployment with minReplicas 4 / maxReplicas 10. When an HPA governs a Deployment, a static replicas value should NOT be pinned — the HPA owns the replica count and will fight a hardcoded value.
conflicts.correct_resolution: Remove the static spec.replicas from the prod Deployment (or leave it unmanaged); the HPA's min/max (4..10) governs scaling. Pinning replicas:5 under an HPA is the conflict to flag.
conflicts.why_it_matters: This is a real-world footgun: replicas pinned under an HPA causes thrash. Must be detected as a cross-object semantic conflict, not just a value diff.

effective_replicas.prod: governed by HPA (min 4 / max 10); static replicas:5 in the patch is a conflict and should not be pinned
